acceptance_targets: [chapter_10_craft_continuity_reva_timeline] PASS if a reader can move through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:13, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:51, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:107, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:159, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:193 and infer one coherent chronology: Reva left when Melie was four, Jin has been carrying that abandonment since then, and the death lie used in this naming matches or is explicitly confronted against the earlier three-month claim. FAIL if those passages still imply both a recent abandonment and a different public death date without acknowledgment.

[chapter_10_prose_jin_voice_abstraction] PASS if those cited passages still communicate Jin's trap but do so through body, object, and situational detail rather than meta terms like 'architecture,' 'fiction,' or generalized moral commentary. FAIL if the revised lines still sound like an essay about lying instead of Jin's physically grounded interiority.

[chapter_10_prose_explanatory_formula_tic] PASS if, when reading the six cited passages, no more than two still rely on the 'the [noun] of a [person] who...' explanatory construction and the other moments register through observable behavior alone. FAIL if that template remains the chapter's default way of telling the reader how to interpret people.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a reader can move through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:13, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:51, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:107, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:159, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:193 and infer one coherent chronology: Reva left when Melie was four, Jin has been carrying that abandonment since then, and the death lie used in this naming matches or is explicitly confronted against the earlier three-month claim. FAIL if those passages still imply both a recent abandonment and a different public death date without acknowledgment.
findings.evidence: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:13 Melie is framed as if her mother's disappearance happened only weeks ago; at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:57 Jin says the death was 'last year'; at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:159 Jin remembers the transit-office lie as a recent event; at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:193 he thinks of 'Three months of managing Melie alone.'
findings.problem: This chapter breaks canon on both the private and public timeline of Reva's disappearance. The continuity sheet fixes the real abandonment as happening when Melie was four and Jin's public death lie as a three-months-earlier claim, but this chapter treats the abandonment as fresh and improvises a different public date. Because Fasa does not confront the earlier public version, the intended interrogation pressure turns into a continuity error.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the abandonment setup beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:13 and the backstory memory beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:159 so Jin's private history reads as a two-year wound, not a three-month one. Then revise the questioning beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:51 and the mirrored follow-up beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:107 so the public lie either stays consistent with the already-established three-month timeline or Fasa explicitly names the contradiction on the page.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if those cited passages still communicate Jin's trap but do so through body, object, and situational detail rather than meta terms like 'architecture,' 'fiction,' or generalized moral commentary. FAIL if the revised lines still sound like an essay about lying instead of Jin's physically grounded interiority.
findings.evidence: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:55 the narration gives Jin 'narrative architecture'; at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:99 it calls the lie 'three layers deep'; at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:145 Melie's expectations become 'the architecture of her world'; at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:163 the narration says 'The lie relied on decency.'
findings.problem: Jin's close-third interior intermittently slips into essayistic, meta-narrative abstraction that does not match the style bible's concrete, body-first private register. These lines sound like an external analyst arranging the scene rather than Jin thinking under humiliation pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the abstract framing at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:55, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:99, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:145, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:163 with objects, bodily cues, and immediate scene logic Jin would actually notice: the missing letter, the catch in his jaw, Melie's face in the children's cluster, the transit form in his hand.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, when reading the six cited passages, no more than two still rely on the 'the [noun] of a [person] who...' explanatory construction and the other moments register through observable behavior alone. FAIL if that template remains the chapter's default way of telling the reader how to interpret people.
findings.evidence: The chapter repeats the same explanatory sentence shape at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:5 ('the weariness of a woman who...'), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:47 ('the cadence of someone who...'), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:145 ('the confusion of a child whose...'), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:199 ('the care of a man whose...'), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:223 ('the adjustment of a man who...'), and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:227 ('the economy of a man who...').
findings.problem: This repeated interpretive formula becomes a visible prose tic. After the third or fourth instance, the narration starts pre-explaining what a gesture means instead of trusting the gesture itself, which flattens otherwise strong scene detail.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary or cut most of the explanatory glosses at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:47, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:199, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:223, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:227. Keep at most two of this sentence shape and let the rest land through action, cadence, and sensory detail without appended decoding.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel operates in the register between domestic realism and the uncanny. The manifestations must be rendered matter-of-factly — not with horror-genre dread, not with fantasy-genre wonder, but with the flat strangeness of an ordinary impossible thing. A four-year-old rubbing her ear in an old woman's gesture IS strange; the prose should let it be strange without managing the reader's response. The families' emotional reactions provide intensity; the narrative voice stays grounded. Commit fully to the strangeness without explaining it away and without escalating it into the supernatural spectacular. Similarly, commit to the difficult emotional material without providing moral commentary, narrator-managed redemption arcs, or reassurance that things will be all right. Jin's shame, Suna's hidden fracture, Gitta's calculated lie, Bett's unperformed grief, Venn's rigidity — none of these should be softened to make the characters more sympathetic. Trust the reader to hold complexity. The novel's refusal to explain the manifestations definitively is a creative risk — resist the pressure to resolve the ambiguity in the final chapters. The families act in the face of genuine uncertainty, and so must the reader.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for this novel's difficult material — a child's anaphylactic reaction, a drowned boy, a stolen infant, the shame of spousal abandonment, coerced participation in ritual, the uncanny experience of watching your child develop another person's traits, the sexual and emotional charge between Arash and Bett — is a craft failure. Each of these situations demands physical and emotional specificity. Summary, softening, moral self-reassurance, and narrator-managed emotional distance all constitute euphemism. A throat swelling shut must be rendered as a throat swelling shut. A man whose wife left must carry the specific texture of that shame, not a generic sadness. A woman naming her dead child must feel the name in her mouth. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for most characters. Uncontracted forms are reserved for Olet's institutional voice, Gitta's careful precision, Venn's formal commands, and Fasa's ritual language. Even these characters should contract in casual or private speech. If two characters are in a scene and both speak without contractions, at least one voice is calibrated wrong. When in doubt, contract.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate intensity levels across consecutive chapters. A crisis chapter must be followed by aftermath, reflection, or quiet — never by another crisis at the same pitch. Vary scene count (1-3 scenes per chapter), dialogue density (the meal chapter should be mostly action and sensory detail; the debate chapter should be dialogue-heavy), and the ratio of interiority to external action. Chapters 3, 9, and 11 should feel spacious and observational. Chapters 10, 13, and 14 should feel compressed and relentless. Chapter 16 is a single sustained scene between two people. Chapter 18 is expansive and polyphonic. No two consecutive chapters should share structural shape, dominant sense, or emotional register.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded, precise, mid-register. Neither lyrical nor stripped. The prose earns beauty through specificity rather than ornamentation, and earns force through precision rather than intensity. Allow images to land without explaining them. Avoid both prestige-literary arabesques and genre-thriller bluntness. The goal is the transparency of good realist prose that becomes uncanny only when the material itself is uncanny — the manifestations should be rendered with the same flatness as a meal or a sleeping arrangement, and the flatness should make them stranger.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating among five POV characters by chapter assignment (Suna, Jin, Fasa, Leni, Venn). The narration sits behind one character's eyes per chapter, with full access to their thoughts and perceptions. The POV character's emotional state colors the prose — Suna's chapters are more cataloguing and load-bearing, Jin's more fragmented and physically grounded, Fasa's more rhythmic and pattern-seeking, Leni's more colloquial and observational, Venn's more declarative and hierarchical. The narrator's baseline voice provides continuity across POV shifts without flattening the differences.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable, following the POV character's internal tempo and the scene's intensity. Medium-length sentences are the default. Compress for crisis and impact — shorter sentences during manifestations, confrontations, and moments of physical fear. Elongate for reflection, beauty, and communal warmth. Avoid sustained barrages in either direction: no thriller-staccato runs longer than three sentences, no lyrical elongation that loses the reader's breath. The prose should feel like variable breathing — natural acceleration and deceleration, with occasional held breath at moments of revelation.
